Tutorial_Airlocks.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "3332F7C94256CB620C99B299EF8D08FB", "Larger <Important>Habitats</> must be connected to other <Important>Structures</> as well as <Important>Platforms</> using <Facility>Airlocks</>.

You can use a <Facility>Door Seal</> to close off unused entrances.

These items can be found in the <Important>Structures</> tab within <Important>Build Mode</>.")

Tutorial_Caverns.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "01C16A704FA77DB429DA3CAF0BE9B6F0", "Within the <Important>Caverns</> of <Important>Aethus</> you are at risk from various hazards, so be careful.

You can come and go via <Important>Cave Entrances</> at any time - these are marked on your <Important>Compass</>.

While <Important>Underground</> you will need to manage two resources - <Health>Health</> and <Air>Air</>.

These are shown in the lower left corner of the screen.

At the exit to the <Important>Surface</>, your <Air>Air Supply</> will recharge.
As you descend deeper into the <Important>Caverns</>, your <Air>Air Supply</> will gradually <Important>decrease</>.

Reaching a level of zero will cause your <Health>Health</> to start degrading.

Your <Air>Air Supply</> level is displayed in <Air>blue</> at the lower left corner of the screen by the 02 icon.

The lights on Maeve's suit will change colour depending on your air supply state - <Yellow>yellow</> to <Health>red</> as your air supply decreases, and <Green>green</> for increasing or steady.

In your exploration you will also <Important>discover areas or craft items</> which can <Important>refill</> your <Air>Air Supply</>, so keep an eye out!

Make sure to return to the <Important>Surface</> or air-supplied areas frequently to refill!

Your <Health>Health</> is displayed at the bottom left of the screen, in <Health>red</> by the heart icon.
It will gradually regenerate over time if you are hurt, as long as you have a stable <Air>Air Supply</>.

If your <Health>Health</> reaches zero you will <Important>collapse</> and be returned to safety on the <Important>Surface</>, but <Important>drop all of your carried items</> (except critical items).

<Important>If you can return to the same location, you can retrieve these items.</>
<Important>You can disable dropping items on passing out in the Settings.</>

As you explore, remember you can use your suit's Scan ability <Cyan>(X)</> to highlight interactable objects and resources within the environment.")

Tutorial_Compass.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "14B0BCDC4131A53955198688C1D5FE1B", "<Important>Points of Interest</> are marked by icons on the <Important>Compass</> at the top of the screen - as you rotate the camera, these icons will move to indicate where things are.

Your constructed <Facility>Habitats</> are also marked by icons on the <Important>Compass</> so that you can always find your way back to the <Important>Outpost</>.

You may need to approach some <Important>Points of Interest</>to discover what they are and permanently mark them for later reference.")

Tutorial_Construction.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "B047037D41490F95B8E31095F2014184", "Building your Outpost is done using Build Mode. You can open Build Mode with (B) to view available construction categories. Many more categories and constructions will become available over time. Facilities are a type of construction which can perform various tasks - allowing you to refine materials, craft or store items, purchase new building plans, and more. When you select something for construction, a 'Ghost' of that object will appear in the world at your cursor location. It can be placed with (LMB) and rotated with (CTRL + MMB Scroll). It will glow blue when in a valid location and orange when it cannot be built. Trying to build while the position is invalid will prompt a notification of the issue. You can see the cost of the construction by hovering over its icon. Constructions that you do not have the required materials to build will be greyed out but can still be hovered to view their information. You can Pin a construction blueprint to the left side of the screen with (RMB), in order to keep track of what materials are required to build it.")

Tutorial_Cooking.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "49C4933747B1081EBD4BE48562FB7219", "The Grill and other similar Facilities allow you to cook and prepare meals and other items which can affect Maeve's Vitality. Interact with the Grill and click the Cooking button in the Info Panel to bring up the Cooking Panel. Recipes for items available for Cooking are shown, along with their required ingredients. You can Pin a Recipe to the left side of the screen with (RMB) in order to keep track of the required ingredients. Click the Recipe you'd like to prepare to start the process. Click multiple times to add items to the Queue. Items you have available for use as ingredients are shown in the right-hand Inventory panel. Ingredients will be automatically taken from your Inventory when a recipe is added to the queue. Items prepared via Cooking will be placed in the Inventory of the Grill.")

Tutorial_Crafting.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "1D9046B84F18057A19A4DEA2921283C3", "The Crafting Bench and other Crafting Facilities allow you to manufacture useful items you will need to progress. Interact with the Crafting Bench and click the Crafting Panel button in the Info Panel to open the Crafting Panel. Plans for items available for crafting are shown, along with their required materials. You can Pin a plan to the left side of the screen with (RMB) in order to keep track of the required materials. Click the Plan you'd like to craft to start the process. Click multiple times to add items to the Queue. Items you have available for use as crafting materials are shown in the right-hand inventory panel. This list includes all items in your own inventory, as well as anything in storage facilities anywhere within your Outpost. Items will be automatically taken from any available inventory, starting with your own, when a Plan is added to the Queue. Items created via Crafting will be placed in the Inventory of the Crafting Bench.")

Tutorial_Customization.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "0DA45B5241D58CDCB3E4CA8EFE329808", "You've unlocked your first <Important>Character Customization element</>!

You can purchase these at the <Facility>Trade Node</> or find them randomly while exploring the world.

Build a <Facility>Customization Kiosk</> facility to customize both <Important>Maeve</> and <Important>Roland</>!")

Tutorial_DemoOutro.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "F16B83484E5964F22024C9A80D6D6FEE", "<Important>Congratulations, you've completed the demo!</>

In the full game, you can explore the vast caverns beneath <Important>Aethus</> across <Important>several different biomes</>.

If you decide to purchase the full game, <Important>you can continue on from your current save game</>.

Please consider <Important>wishlisting</> on <Important>Steam</> if you had fun, and I'd love to hear your thoughts on the game by joining the <Important>Discord</>.

There are links for both these things on the <Important>Pause Menu</> <Cyan>(ESC)</>.

<Important>Thanks so much for playing!</>")

Tutorial_DiscoveredPlans.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "5C32D32D4A8D72F59D59D1ABAC96858A", "When you discover Construction Blueprints or Crafting Plans you\'ve already purchased at the Trade Node you will get back half the Credits you spent.\r\n\r\nDon\'t hesitate to purchase from the Planetary Exchange Network and explore, you\'ll never be too out of pocket!")

Tutorial_Dying.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "F387FE2040F3D7AF6EB0A8AA75634A04", "If your <Health>Health</> reaches zero you will <Important>collapse</>.

You will be returned to the <Important>Surface</>, but <Important>drop all of your carried items</>.

<Facility>If you return to the same location, you can retrieve these items.</>

Use the <Important>Compass</> at the top of the screen to locate your item box.")

Tutorial_Habitats.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "EC31DFF847250418CF52FFABA508EFE0", "<Important>Habitats</> are buildable structures which allow for the placement of <Important>Facilities</>, as well as furnishings and decorations, within them.

There are many shapes and sizes of <Important>Habitat</> you'll use to build up your <Important>Outpost</>.

Constructing <Important>Habitats</>, just like <Important>Facilities</>, is done with <Important>Build Mode</> <Cyan>(B)</>.")

Tutorial_Interaction.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "2D186BD14A25AF992DFF88A7CC899B34", "Many objects in the world can be interacted with. You can hover over objects and use (LMB) to interact. You can activate the Scan ability of Maeve's suit with (X) to highlight interactable objects and resources within the environment.")

Tutorial_Intro.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "912304134EA99A007BB74AB766354EF3", "You can move around the world with (W)/(A)/(S)/(D). Auto-Run is enabled by default. You can toggle this in Settings. Hold (SHIFT) to walk more slowly, and (SPACE) to jump. You can orbit the camera by holding (MMB) and moving the mouse, or by using Q/E to rotate the camera. You can zoom the camera using (MMB Scroll).")

Tutorial_Inventory.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "7DF02B374A4D6FD12E3FE08492F43B1E", "You can open and view your Inventory and its contents with (TAB). Walking over loose items in the world will add them to your Inventory. These items can also be pulled towards you when in range by hovering them and using (LMB). Hovering an item in your Inventory will show more information about it Consume items in your Inventory with (MMB).")

Tutorial_Market.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "5EF6CF804DFC21E96DF588BD58762E07", "You've unlocked the ability to access the ARC Market! You can do so through the Trade Node. The ARC Planetary Exchange Network - the Market - allows you to sell resources you've gathered for Credits , and use those Credits to purchase Construction Blueprints for your Outpost as well as new Crafting Plans. As you progress in the Resource Acquisition Partnership Program, visible in the upper left of the UI, you will unlock new blueprints and plans.. Interact (LMB) with the Trade Node and open the Market Panel. Items and resources that you are carrying are shown in the list on the right side of the Market Panel. You can use the + and - buttons to select how many of each type of item you'd like to Sell to ARC. Clicking (LMB) the item button itself will select all of that item for sale, and clicking it again will deselect all of that item. All selected items, and their total value, are shown in the central panel. Use the 'SELL' button to sell all selected items to ARC and gain the indicated amount of Credits . The left side of the Market Panel contains Crafting Plans and Construction Blueprints. Clicking (LMB) the relevant header button for each category will display those items that are available for sale in a list. Clicking (LMB) an item will select it for purchase, and display information about it as well as its cost in Credits in the central panel. You can filter for specific items using the search box above the category buttons. Use the 'PURCHASE' button to purchase the selected Plan or Blueprint, for the indicated amount of Credits .")

Tutorial_Mining.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "52350813486592F00E877E84DF63E11A", "Many objects are susceptible to Roland's Mining Laser. Interacting (LMB) with such an object begins to channel Energy from the laser into it. When the Energy reaches a critical level, it discharges in an explosion and blows apart the object. Some objects dissipate more Energy than others, so multiple laser blasts may be required. Make sure to channel the Energy into the same location to continue to build it up!")

Tutorial_Nodes.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "9983577245FB3F033674FD8310ED22DA", "Nodes are clumps of Resources which can be broken apart with Roland's Mining Laser. You can highlight mineable Nodes in the environment by using Maeve's suit's Scan Ability (X). Interact (LMB) with a Node to begin channeling Energy into it with each Laser blast. Due to Aethus' unstable geology, Nodes will re-appear over time and may emerge in different locations than previously! Each shot from his Mining Laser drains Roland's Energy - this is shown by the fullness of the circle that makes up your cursor. Energy will recharge to full over time but you cannot mine when it is too low. Different types of Node will require different amounts of Energy to fracture - you will not be able to mine everything immediately. You will be able to purchase Upgrades for Roland's Energy level in the future.")

Tutorial_Objectives.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D6DDC8CE4080871BDFAAAA955EFBAC92", "<Important>Tasks</> are shown in the top-left of the screen - you can hover over this panel to view them or scroll the list if needed.

<Important>Maeve</> will automatically record events and discoveries in the <Important>Journal</> <Cyan>(J)</> as you play.

Refer to your <Important>Tasks</> and <Important>Journal</> if you ever get stuck and aren't sure how to proceed.")

Tutorial_Power.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "8201AE8441AC9D4616F416A1E13DE77D", "Many Facilities require Power to function. This is shown for each blueprint in Build Mode and is also displayed in a construction's Info Panel. Power-producing Facilities like the RTG you can now build provide Power across the entire Outpost. The Power level of your Outpost can be viewed on the right of the Build Mode UI and hovered for more detailed info. If the Power level drops below 0 then all powered Facilities will cease functioning.")

Tutorial_PowerSlots.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "CEF9139543141DFEDD3BD1918E2A8128", "To access Power , Facilities take up Facility Slots . Facility blueprints display the amount of Facility Slots they require in Build Mode, and once constructed, Facilities display how many they are occupying in their Info Panel. Habitats display the amount of Facility Slots they provide in the same ways. Slots with a lightning bolt icon are occupied, and empty slots have no icon. Placing a Facility within a Habitat automatically connects it to Power , and occupies free Facility Slots . Dismantling a Facility will free up any Facility Slots it was occupying.")

Tutorial_Refining.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "1C472DD84C0BA665F5B9DE906A59B928", "Refining allows you to turn one or more types of resources into others. Interacting (LMB) with constructed objects will open the Info Panel, in the bottom right of the screen. (RMB) or (ESC) closes this Info Panel (and most other types of UI). You can transfer resources from your inventory to the facility, and back again, by using: (LMB) will transfer one item at a time. (RMB) will transfer the entire stack at once, or as much as will fit. Items in a Facility's Inventory, will be processed automatically using enabled Refining Recipes when the requirements have been met. Facilities may have multiple Refining Recipes available, and you will unlock new Recipes during the course of the game. You can disable and re-enable Recipes from the scrollable list by clicking them. You can also Pin (RMB) a Recipe to the side of the screen with the checkbox in order to keep track of the materials required.")

Tutorial_RestoringFunctionality.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "38B3BF91413C6C4136342BB80FF49636", "Certain objects in the world require <Important>specific items</> to be present in their <Important>Inventory</> in order to function.

Items required to restore functionality are shown in the <Important>Inventory</> of the object as <Important>greyed-out icons with a border</>.

You will need to <Important>find</> or <Important>craft</> these items and <Important>deposit</> them within the object to <Important>restore functionality</>.

In the case of this <Important>elevator</>, acquiring a <Item>Power Cell</> and adding it to the <Important>Required Items section</> of the elevator will restore power and allow you to <Important>quickly ascend or descend from this location</>.")

Tutorial_Snapping.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "3572FE1F4CF3B1E93EDD48AA3A988330", "You'll need to build an <Facility>Access Platform</> to get up some newly constructed <Important> Habitats</>.

Constructions like <Important>Habitats</> and <Important>Platforms</> will <Important>'snap'</> to certain locations on others, like doorways, so that you can <Important>easily connect them together</>.

In this way, you can create an interconnected set of <Important>Habitats</>.

Hover the <Important>'ghost'</> of a snappable <Important>Construction</> over a snap point on another, like a door, to see this in action.


If you need to move any of your existing <Important>Habitats</> or <Important>Facilities</>, simply select it in <Important>Build Mode</> and press <Important>Move</>.

You can also <Important>Deconstruct</> anything in the same way using the relevant button, and <Important>will always receive back the full construction materials</>.

Some constructions with multiple entrances can have their snap location changed with <Cyan>(V)</>.
You can temporarily disable snapping by holding down <Cyan>(CTRL)</> while moving a construction blueprint.")

Tutorial_Tectonics.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "A57142AD4347681F099A818908001FA3", "The active geological activity of this part of <Important>Aethus</> causes frequent shifts in the underground.

Resource <Important>Nodes</> may appear, disappear or change type entirely as these tremors occur.

It is recommended you revisit previously explored areas after tremors occur, if you are in need of <Important>resources</>.")

Tutorial_Terraforming.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "9587E1FD445421289D0CCFABA22346F2", "<Item>Terraforming Charges</> allow you to clear <Important>Outcrops</> from the surface to make way for your <Important>Outpost</>.

Different <Item>Charges</> will clear different <Important>Outcrops</>.
You can <Cyan>hover the cursor</> over an <Important>Outcrop</> to see a pop-up of what type of <Item>Charge</> is required to clear it.

<Item>Charges</> have a radius of clearance so it can be worth trying to clear multiple <Important>Outcrops</> at once.")

Tutorial_Themes.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "DC0AB2FF45F36933936CB9BD9C4DF8B6", "You've unlocked a new <Important>Outpost Theme</>!

Select any <Facility>Habitat</> or <Facility>Facility</> using <Important>Build Mode</> then select the <Important>Theme</> category and choose one.

Applying an <Important>Outpost Theme</> to a <Facility>Habitat</> will apply it automatically to all contained <Facility>Facilities</>.")

Tutorial_TimeOfDay.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "7F7ED2CE4DAB45EC2E2A4486CE0A2DF6", "Time passes slowly on <Important>Aethus</>, but day will eventually turn to night as the sun sets.
The current <Important>Time of Day</> is displayed in the lower-left of the screen.

As darkness descends, it will naturally become <Important>more difficult</> to expand your <Important>Outpost</>.

You can use <Facility>Beds</> to <Important>Sleep</> and pass the time to a desired section of the day by interacting with them.")

Tutorial_Upgrades.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "7E2E16B64EB35F7EB0B4CAA1375FA214", "You've unlocked the ability to purchase Upgrades! Upgrades require Credits to purchase, but grant powerful bonuses to your Suit or Roland's Laser, allowing you to delve further into the underground and mine new resources. Interact (LMB) with the Trade Node and select the Upgrade Panel. You have enough Credits to purchase your first Upgrade - Mining Laser Crystal I. This will upgrade Roland's Laser with more power, allowing you to mine Kalynite Ore Nodes. You can purchase this Upgrade by simply holding (LMB) on its icon in the Upgrade Panel. Upgrades may require other Upgrades to be owned before they can be purchased. These are shown as lines in the Upgrade Panel. Purchasing an Upgrade allows you to access the next in the 'chain' - for example, once you purchase its predecessor, you'll be able to purchase Mining Laser Crystal II when you have enough Credits ! You'll want to continue purchasing Upgrades in order to keep progressing deeper into the world.")

Tutorial_Vitality.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265:
NSLOCTEXT("", "D5E689484FF33D9BDBA3A7B3C84581F7", "<Important>Maeve</> will become hungry and thirsty over time, and gain positive <Important>Status Effects</> depending on the status of her <Important>Vitality</>, comprised of <Energy>Stamina</>, <Nutrition>Nutrition</> and <Thirst>Hydration</>.

These stats are represented at the bottom left of the screen. Hover over the icons to view more information about each stat.

You can find or craft <Important>Consumable</> items which will affect Maeve's <Important>Vitality</> in different ways - experiment with them to find your favourite.

You can view <Important>Status Effects</> affecting Maeve as icons near the bottom of the screen. 
Hover over one to view more info about it.

<Important>Maeve will never die or lose health if you neglect her Vitality, unless Negative Vitality Effects are enabled in Settings.</>")

Tutorial_Weight.JournalEntryText_25_10D44AC44144EC853265: NSLOCTEXT("", "FC63B4DD44DCDFC4E881F4945D8EF74B", "Maeve can only carry a certain amount of Weight at one time, made up of all the items currently carried in the Inventory. Different items have different Weights - hovering over an item shows its Tooltip, which shows the individual weight of one item, and the amount of that item in the 'stack'. The top-right of the Inventory panel shows Maeve's current vs maximum carryable Weight. You won't be able to pick up new items when Maeve has reached her maximum Weight limit. You will be able to obtain Upgrades to increase Maeve's maximum carrying Weight later. In the mean-time, you can utilize Storage Containers. Storage Containers allow you to store items in your Habitats for later use. Different Storage Containers can hold different amounts of items, based on their total Weight. You can see the maximum held Weight of the Facility in its Info Panel. You will be able to unlock larger Storage Containers as you progress through the game. You can deposit items into a Storage Containers by Interacting with it like any other Facility, then using (LMB) or (RMB) on the item's icon in your Inventory, to transfer one item or the whole stack, respectively. The Info Panel of the destination must be open in order to deposit items. Take items from a Storage Containers in the same way, by using (LMB) or (RMB) on the desired item in the Inventory section of the Info Panel of the Facility. One special aspect of Storage Containers is that items within them will automatically be available for use in Crafting, alongside any items in your own Inventory.")
